company_core.name: Genspark
company_core.description: The main product is an AI-driven search engine that utilizes advanced algorithms to enhance search capabilities and improve the relevance of results. Additionally, it incorporates an AI agent framework aimed at increasing user engagement with the content.

founders.name: Ray Zhong
founders.background:
Ray Zhong served as Chief Architect of DuerOS at Baidu, Inc. from 2017 to 2023, where he led the development of the conversational AI platform. Prior to that, he was a staff software engineer at Google for a decade, contributing to core search ranking algorithms and the Google Assistant's home automation features. He holds a Master's degree in Mathematics and Computer Science from Sun Yat-sen University.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/ray-zhong-a3735627, x.com/ruradium

founders.name: Lenjoy Lin
founders.background:
Lenjoy Lin has over 12 years of experience in engineering leadership, having served as Head of Engineering for TikTok Ads Measurement & Targeting at ByteDance, where he founded and scaled the department. Prior to that, he led the Consumer Personalization Engineering Team at Houzz and was a Tech Lead for Homefeed Ads at Pinterest. He began his career as a Staff Software Engineer at YouTube, where he was a founding member responsible for rebuilding the ads serving and quality stack. Lenjoy holds a Bachelor's degree in Mathematics from Zhejiang University.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/lenjoy, x.com/lenjoy

founders.name: Kay Zhu
founders.background:
Kay Zhu is a seasoned technology leader with a strong background in AI and search technologies. Prior to co-founding Genspark, she served as CTO at Xiaodu Technology, where she initiated the company from the ground up and secured Series B financing, achieving a $5.1 billion valuation. Before that, she was the Chief Architect of Baidu Search, leading innovations in AI and launching the world's first DNN ranking model. Her experience also includes a role as a Staff Software Engineer at Google, where she contributed to core search ranking and web anti-spam technologies.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/kay-zhu-522b275

founders.name: Eric Jing
founders.background:
Eric Jing is a seasoned entrepreneur and technology executive with a strong track record in AI and search technologies. He co-founded and served as CEO of Genspark in 2023, and previously led Xiaodu Technology, Inc. as CEO from 2020 to 2023, where he successfully closed Series B financing at a $5.5 billion valuation. Prior to that, he was the Chief Product Manager of Search and Corporate VP at Baidu, Inc., driving innovations in web search and AI products, and he also held a key role as Principal Development Manager of Bing Search at Microsoft, where he contributed to the launch of Bing.com.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/eric-jing-5397a1b

agent_output.markdown:
# Genspark | Meeting Brief
*2026-03-03*

## TL;DR
Genspark is an AI-driven search engine and agent framework company that has raised $435M total, including a $275M Series B in November 2025. The company is growing aggressively — headcount up 55% in 90 days and web traffic approaching 15M monthly visits. The founding team carries exceptional pedigree from Baidu, Google, ByteDance, and Microsoft, making this a high-conviction bet on experienced operators.

---

## Company Overview
Genspark builds an AI-powered search engine using advanced algorithms to improve result relevance, paired with an AI agent framework designed to deepen user engagement. Founded in 2023, the company is B2B-oriented and sits at the intersection of enterprise productivity, AI, and search. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)

- **Stage:** Series B [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)
- **Total Raised:** $435M [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)
- **Headcount:** 45 (up 55.2% in 90 days) [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)
- **Monthly Web Traffic:** ~14.9M (up 16.3% in 30 days) [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)

---

## Key Points
• **Exceptional founding team with direct search and AI DNA.** Eric Jing (CEO) was Corporate VP and Chief Product Manager of Search at Baidu and a Principal Development Manager on Bing at Microsoft. Kay Zhu (CTO) was Chief Architect of Baidu Search and previously CTO at Xiaodu Technology, which reached a $5.1B valuation. Ray Zhong was Chief Architect of DuerOS at Baidu and a staff engineer at Google working on core search ranking. Lenjoy Lin led TikTok Ads Measurement & Targeting engineering at ByteDance. This is arguably one of the most search-credentialed founding teams in the market. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)

• **Massive capital raised relative to headcount.** $435M raised with only 45 employees implies an extraordinarily high capital-per-employee ratio — either significant runway is being preserved, or substantial spend is going toward compute/infrastructure rather than headcount. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)[[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)

• **Series B closed November 2025 at $275M.** Investors include Emergence Capital, LG Technology Ventures, SBI Investment, Lanchi Ventures, and UpHonest Capital. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)

• **Traffic scale is notable for a 2023-founded company.** ~15M monthly visits with 16% month-over-month growth suggests real user traction, though the B2B tag raises questions about whether this traffic is converting to enterprise revenue. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)[[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)

• **Headcount growth of 55% in 90 days signals active scaling.** At 45 people, the company is still small — this hiring pace suggests they are in a rapid build-out phase post-Series B. [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)

---

## Recent Developments
• Closed a $275M Series B round on November 20, 2025, bringing total funding to $435M. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)
• Headcount surged 55.2% over the past 90 days, indicating aggressive post-funding hiring. [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)
• Web traffic grew 16.3% in the past 30 days, reaching approximately 14.9M monthly visits. [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)

---

## Signals & Trends
• **Hiring:** Rapid team expansion (+55.2% in 90 days) from a still-lean 45-person base — watch for where they are hiring (engineering vs. GTM) as a signal of product vs. commercialization focus. [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)
• **Traffic:** 14.9M monthly visits with continued growth is a strong signal of product-market fit at the consumer/prosumer level, though alignment with B2B positioning warrants scrutiny. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)[[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)
• **Funding velocity:** Two rounds implied within the $435M total at Series B stage suggests investors are moving quickly and competitively to back this team. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)

---

## For This Meeting
• **Revenue & monetization:** With $435M raised and ~15M monthly visitors, what does the revenue model look like? Is there enterprise ARR, and if so, what is it?
• **B2B vs. consumer tension:** The company is tagged B2B, but 15M monthly web visits suggests significant consumer usage — clarify the go-to-market focus and whether consumer traction is a funnel into enterprise or a separate motion.
• **Capital deployment:** What is the $275M Series B being used for — compute, hiring, GTM? The high capital-to-headcount ratio needs explanation.
• **Competitive differentiation:** The AI search space is intensely competitive. Probe specifically what the AI agent framework does that incumbents (not named in data) cannot replicate quickly.
• **Founder titles:** Only Eric Jing (CEO) and Kay Zhu (CTO) have explicit titles confirmed. Clarify roles of Ray Zhong and Lenjoy Lin within the current organization. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/54909419)
• **Risk — team size vs. ambition:** 45 people building a search engine and agent framework at this scale is very lean. Execution risk is real; probe org structure and key hires planned.
